content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

October 18, 2007

Steven G. Churchwell
400 Capitol Mall, Suite 2400
Sacramento, CA 95814-4428

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-07-165

Dear Mr. Churchwell:

This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the mass mailing provisions of the Political Reform Act.¹ Because your request seeks advice based on a mailing that has already been sent, we are declining to provide advice in this matter. The Fair Political Practices Commission does not render advice based on past conduct. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A).) We therefore are withdrawing your request.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel

By: Heather M. Rowan
Counsel, Legal Division

HMR:jgl

___________________________

¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

sections.conclusion: The Fair Political Practices Commission does not render advice based on past conduct. (Regulation 18329(bXBXA).) The request is therefore being withdrawn.
sections.question_synthetic: The requester sought advice regarding mass mailing provisions of the Political Reform Act related to a mailing that had already been sent.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC is declining to provide advice because the request is based on past conduct (a mailing already sent), and the FPPC does not render advice on completed actions.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a withdrawal letter. No traditional QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections are present, but the substance of the request and the FPPC's rationale for declining are clearly stated in the body of the letter. The document is well-structured despite the lack of formal section headers.
